# HQ - Harness Orchestrator & Dashboard

A native desktop application for managing AI agent development sessions.

## Stack

- **Tauri 2.x** - Native desktop shell
- **React 19** - UI framework
- **PatternFly 6** - Enterprise UI components
- **@zazcart/tools** - State management

## Development

```bash
# Install dependencies
bun install

# Run frontend only
bun run dev

# Run with Tauri (full app)
bun tauri dev

# Build for production
bun tauri build
```

## Harness

This project uses a harness for persistent context across AI sessions.

- `.harness/progress.txt` - Session history
- `.harness/features.json` - Feature requirements

See `docs/HQ_SPEC.md` for full specification.

content.content:
1→# HQ - Harness Orchestrator & Dashboard
     2→
     3→## Vision
     4→
     5→**HQ** (Headquarters) is a native desktop application that provides a visual dashboard for managing AI agent development sessions. It enables developers to orchestrate, monitor, and control multiple Claude Code sessions across different projects without using a terminal.
     6→
     7→## Core Philosophy
     8→
     9→> "A Single Pane of Glass for AI-Assisted Development"
    10→
    11→HQ transforms the way developers interact with long-running AI agents by providing:
    12→- **Visual Kanban boards** for feature tracking
    13→- **Real-time logs** from all running processes
    14→- **PTY-based process control** with full interactivity
    15→- **Multi-project management** via tabs
    16→- **Harness state visualization**
    17→
    18→---
    19→
    20→## Architecture Overview
    21→
    22→```
    23→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
    24→│                         HQ Desktop App                               │
    25→│                    (Tauri + React 19 + PatternFly 6)                 │
    26→├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
    27→│                                                                      │
    28→│  ┌────────────────┐  ┌────────────────┐  ┌────────────────────────┐ │
    29→│  │   Tab Bar      │  │   Kanban View   │  │   Logs Panel           │ │
    30→│  │   (Projects)   │  │   (Features)    │  │   (Real-time PTY)      │ │
    31→│  └────────────────┘  └────────────────┘  └────────────────────────┘ │
    32→│                                                                      │
    33→├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
    34→│                       State Management Layer                         │
    35→│                  (@zazcart/tools state-machine)                    │
    36→├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
    37→│                       Rust Backend (Tauri)                           │
    38→│                                                                      │
    39→│  ┌─────────────────┐  ┌─────────────────┐  ┌─────────────────────┐  │
    40→│  │ PTY Controller  │  │ File Watcher    │  │ IPC Commands        │  │
    41→│  │ (portable-pty)  │  │ (notify crate)  │  │ (Tauri invoke)      │  │
    42→│  └─────────────────┘  └─────────────────┘  └─────────────────────┘  │
    43→│                                                                      │
    44→├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
    45→│                     External Processes                               │
    46→│                                                                      │
    47→│  ┌─────────────────┐  ┌─────────────────┐  ┌─────────────────────┐  │
    48→│  │ Claude Code #1  │  │ Claude Code #2  │  │ Other Processes     │  │
    49→│  │ (Project A)     │  │ (Project B)     │  │ (servers, tests)    │  │
    50→│  └─────────────────┘  └─────────────────┘  └─────────────────────┘  │
    51→│                                                                      │
    52→└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
    53→```
    54→
    55→---
    56→
    57→## Technology Stack
    58→
    59→| Layer                | Technology          | Version | Purpose                              |
    60→|----------------------|---------------------|---------|--------------------------------------|
    61→| **Desktop Runtime**  | Tauri               | 2.x     | Native app shell, Rust backend       |
    62→| **Frontend**         | React               | 19.x    | UI framework                         |
    63→| **UI Components**    | PatternFly          | 6.x     | Enterprise UI (chat, terminal, logs) |
    64→| **State Management** | @zazcart/tools      | 0.1.0   | State machine + schema validation    |
    65→| **PTY Emulation**    | portable-pty (Rust) | latest  | Process spawning and control         |
    66→| **File Watching**    | notify (Rust)       | latest  | Real-time harness file changes       |
    67→| **IPC**              | Tauri Commands      | -       | Frontend ↔ Backend communication     |
    68→
    69→### Why PatternFly 6?
    70→
    71→PatternFly provides ready-made components for our use cases:
    72→- **Chat/AI components** - For agent communication UI
    73→- **Log viewer** - Real-time log display with ANSI support
    74→- **Terminal** - Embedded terminal views
    75→- **Tabs** - Multi-project navigation
    76→- **Cards/Panels** - Kanban board layout
    77→- **Dark theme** - Built-in dark mode support
    78→
    79→---
    80→
    81→## Feature Specifications
    82→
    83→### F001: Multi-Project Tab Management
    84→
    85→**Description:** Open and manage multiple projects simultaneously via tabs.
    86→
    87→**UI Elements:**
    88→- Tab bar at top (PatternFly Tabs)
    89→- New tab button (+)
    90→- Close tab button (X)
    91→- Tab context menu (rename, close others, close all)
    92→
    93→**Behavior:**
    94→- Each tab represents a project directory
    95→- Tab title shows project name (from package.json or folder name)
    96→- Active tab is highlighted
    97→- Tabs are reorderable via drag-and-drop
    98→
    99→### F002: Kanban Board View
   100→
   101→**Description:** Visual kanban board for feature tracking within a project.
   102→
   103→**Columns:**
   104→```
   105→BACKLOG → TODO → DOING → REVIEW → QA → DONE
   106→```
   107→
   108→**Card Structure:**
   109→- Feature ID (e.g., F001)
   110→- Title
   111→- Description (truncated)
   112→- Status badge
   113→- Priority indicator
   114→- Click to expand details
   115→
   116→**Data Source:**
   117→- Reads from `.harness/features.json`
   118→- Auto-updates when file changes (via file watcher)
   119→- Status mapped to columns:
   120→  - `passes: false` + not started → BACKLOG
   121→  - `passes: false` + priority → TODO
   122→  - `passes: false` + in_progress → DOING
   123→  - `passes: false` + needs_review → REVIEW
   124→  - `passes: false` + testing → QA
   125→  - `passes: true` → DONE
   126→
   127→### F003: Real-Time Log Panel
   128→
   129→**Description:** Live output from running processes (Claude Code, servers, etc.)
   130→
   131→**Features:**
   132→- PatternFly LogViewer component
   133→- ANSI color support
   134→- Timestamp prefix
   135→- Process filter
   136→- Search within logs
   137→- Clear button
   138→- Auto-scroll toggle
   139→
   140→**Technical:**
   141→- PTY streams → Tauri events → React state
   142→- Max 10,000 lines per process (ring buffer)
   143→
   144→### F004: PTY Process Controller
   145→
   146→**Description:** Spawn, control, and interact with shell processes.
   147→
   148→**Capabilities:**
   149→- Start new process (e.g., `claude` command)
   150→- Send keystrokes (including Ctrl+C, Ctrl+D)
   151→- Resize PTY on window resize
   152→- Kill process
   153→- Restart process
   154→
   155→**Rust Implementation:**
   156→```rust
   157→use portable_pty::{CommandBuilder, PtySize, native_pty_system};
   158→
   159→fn spawn_process(cmd: &str, cols: u16, rows: u16) -> Result<PtyPair> {
   160→    let pty_system = native_pty_system();
   161→    let pair = pty_system.openpty(PtySize { rows, cols, .. })?;
   162→    let cmd = CommandBuilder::new(cmd);
   163→    pair.slave.spawn_command(cmd)?;
   164→    Ok(pair)
   165→}
   166→```
   167→
   168→### F005: Harness State Visualization
   169→
   170→**Description:** Display current harness state visually.
   171→
   172→**Components:**
   173→- Progress summary card
   174→  - Features completed / total
   175→  - Current session info
   176→  - Last commit
   177→- Active feature highlight
   178→- Session timeline (from progress.txt)
   179→
   180→### F006: Project Selector Dialog
   181→
   182→**Description:** Open new project from file system.
   183→

# HQ - Harness Orchestrator & Dashboard

## Vision

**HQ** (Headquarters) is a native desktop application that provides a visual dashboard for managing AI agent development sessions. It enables developers to orchestrate, monitor, and control multiple Claude Code sessions across different projects without using a terminal.

## Core Philosophy

> "A Single Pane of Glass for AI-Assisted Development"

HQ transforms the way developers interact with long-running AI agents by providing:
- **Visual Kanban boards** for feature tracking
- **Real-time logs** from all running processes
- **PTY-based process control** with full interactivity
- **Multi-project management** via tabs
- **Harness state visualization**

---

## Architecture Overview

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                         HQ Desktop App                               │
│                    (Tauri + React 19 + PatternFly 6)                 │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                      │
│  ┌────────────────┐  ┌────────────────┐  ┌────────────────────────┐ │
│  │   Tab Bar      │  │   Kanban View   │  │   Logs Panel           │ │
│  │   (Projects)   │  │   (Features)    │  │   (Real-time PTY)      │ │
│  └────────────────┘  └────────────────┘  └────────────────────────┘ │
│                                                                      │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                       State Management Layer                         │
│                  (@zazcart/tools state-machine)                    │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                       Rust Backend (Tauri)                           │
│                                                                      │
│  ┌─────────────────┐  ┌─────────────────┐  ┌─────────────────────┐  │
│  │ PTY Controller  │  │ File Watcher    │  │ IPC Commands        │  │
│  │ (portable-pty)  │  │ (notify crate)  │  │ (Tauri invoke)      │  │
│  └─────────────────┘  └─────────────────┘  └─────────────────────┘  │
│                                                                      │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                     External Processes                               │
│                                                                      │
│  ┌─────────────────┐  ┌─────────────────┐  ┌─────────────────────┐  │
│  │ Claude Code #1  │  │ Claude Code #2  │  │ Other Processes     │  │
│  │ (Project A)     │  │ (Project B)     │  │ (servers, tests)    │  │
│  └─────────────────┘  └─────────────────┘  └─────────────────────┘  │
│                                                                      │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## Technology Stack

| Layer                | Technology          | Version | Purpose                              |
|----------------------|---------------------|---------|--------------------------------------|
| **Desktop Runtime**  | Tauri               | 2.x     | Native app shell, Rust backend       |
| **Frontend**         | React               | 19.x    | UI framework                         |
| **UI Components**    | PatternFly          | 6.x     | Enterprise UI (chat, terminal, logs) |
| **State Management** | @zazcart/tools      | 0.1.0   | State machine + schema validation    |
| **PTY Emulation**    | portable-pty (Rust) | latest  | Process spawning and control         |
| **File Watching**    | notify (Rust)       | latest  | Real-time harness file changes       |
| **IPC**              | Tauri Commands      | -       | Frontend ↔ Backend communication     |

### Why PatternFly 6?

PatternFly provides ready-made components for our use cases:
- **Chat/AI components** - For agent communication UI
- **Log viewer** - Real-time log display with ANSI support
- **Terminal** - Embedded terminal views
- **Tabs** - Multi-project navigation
- **Cards/Panels** - Kanban board layout
- **Dark theme** - Built-in dark mode support

---

## Feature Specifications

### F001: Multi-Project Tab Management

**Description:** Open and manage multiple projects simultaneously via tabs.

**UI Elements:**
- Tab bar at top (PatternFly Tabs)
- New tab button (+)
- Close tab button (X)
- Tab context menu (rename, close others, close all)

**Behavior:**
- Each tab represents a project directory
- Tab title shows project name (from package.json or folder name)
- Active tab is highlighted
- Tabs are reorderable via drag-and-drop

### F002: Kanban Board View

**Description:** Visual kanban board for feature tracking within a project.

**Columns:**
```
BACKLOG → TODO → DOING → REVIEW → QA → DONE
```

**Card Structure:**
- Feature ID (e.g., F001)
- Title
- Description (truncated)
- Status badge
- Priority indicator
- Click to expand details

**Data Source:**
- Reads from `.harness/features.json`
- Auto-updates when file changes (via file watcher)
- Status mapped to columns:
  - `passes: false` + not started → BACKLOG
  - `passes: false` + priority → TODO
  - `passes: false` + in_progress → DOING
  - `passes: false` + needs_review → REVIEW
  - `passes: false` + testing → QA
  - `passes: true` → DONE

### F003: Real-Time Log Panel

**Description:** Live output from running processes (Claude Code, servers, etc.)

**Features:**
- PatternFly LogViewer component
- ANSI color support
- Timestamp prefix
- Process filter
- Search within logs
- Clear button
- Auto-scroll toggle

**Technical:**
- PTY streams → Tauri events → React state
- Max 10,000 lines per process (ring buffer)

### F004: PTY Process Controller

**Description:** Spawn, control, and interact with shell processes.

**Capabilities:**
- Start new process (e.g., `claude` command)
- Send keystrokes (including Ctrl+C, Ctrl+D)
- Resize PTY on window resize
- Kill process
- Restart process

**Rust Implementation:**
```rust
use portable_pty::{CommandBuilder, PtySize, native_pty_system};

fn spawn_process(cmd: &str, cols: u16, rows: u16) -> Result<PtyPair> {
    let pty_system = native_pty_system();
    let pair = pty_system.openpty(PtySize { rows, cols, .. })?;
    let cmd = CommandBuilder::new(cmd);
    pair.slave.spawn_command(cmd)?;
    Ok(pair)
}
```

### F005: Harness State Visualization

**Description:** Display current harness state visually.

**Components:**
- Progress summary card
  - Features completed / total
  - Current session info
  - Last commit
- Active feature highlight
- Session timeline (from progress.txt)

### F006: Project Selector Dialog

**Description:** Open new project from file system.

**UI:**
- PatternFly Modal
- Recent projects list
- Browse button (native file picker via Tauri)
- Validate project (check for .harness folder)

### F007: Settings Panel

**Description:** Configure HQ preferences.

**Settings:**
- Theme (light/dark/system)
- Log buffer size
- Auto-start Claude Code
- Default shell
- Font size

---

## State Schema (using @zazcart/tools)

```typescript
import { Type, Static } from 'typebox';
import { createStore } from '@zazcart/tools';

// Project Schema
const ProjectSchema = Type.Object({
  id: Type.String(),
  path: Type.String(),
  name: Type.String(),
  harness: Type.Optional(Type.Object({
    progressFile: Type.String(),
    featuresFile: Type.String(),
  })),
});

// Feature Schema (from harness)
const FeatureSchema = Type.Object({
  id: Type.String(),
  description: Type.String(),
  category: Type.String(),
  passes: Type.Boolean(),
  steps: Type.Array(Type.String()),
});

// Process Schema
const ProcessSchema = Type.Object({
  id: Type.String(),
  projectId: Type.String(),
  command: Type.String(),
  status: Type.Union([
    Type.Literal('running'),
    Type.Literal('stopped'),
    Type.Literal('error'),
  ]),
  pid: Type.Optional(Type.Number()),
});

// App State
const AppStateSchema = Type.Object({
  activeTab: Type.String(),
  theme: Type.Union([
    Type.Literal('light'),
    Type.Literal('dark'),
    Type.Literal('system'),
  ]),
  projects: Type.Record(Type.String(), ProjectSchema),
  projectOrder: Type.Array(Type.String()),
  features: Type.Record(Type.String(), Type.Array(FeatureSchema)),
  processes: Type.Record(Type.String(), ProcessSchema),
});

type AppState = Static<typeof AppStateSchema>;
```

---

## Tauri Commands (IPC)

```typescript
// Frontend → Backend commands
interface TauriCommands {
  'project:open': (path: string) => Promise<Project>;
  'project:close': (id: string) => Promise<void>;
  'pty:spawn': (projectId: string, cmd: string) => Promise<string>;
  'pty:write': (processId: string, data: string) => Promise<void>;
  'pty:resize': (processId: string, cols: number, rows: number) => Promise<void>;
  'pty:kill': (processId: string) => Promise<void>;
  'harness:read_features': (path: string) => Promise<Feature[]>;
  'harness:watch': (projectPath: string) => Promise<void>;
  'fs:select_directory': () => Promise<string | null>;
}

// Backend → Frontend events
interface TauriEvents {
  'pty:output': { processId: string; data: string };
  'pty:exit': { processId: string; code: number };
  'harness:changed': { projectPath: string; file: string };
}
```

---

## Directory Structure

```
~/antonio/HQ/
├── .claude/
│   ├── settings.json
│   └── hooks/
├── .harness/
│   ├── progress.txt
│   └── features.json
├── docs/
│   └── HQ_SPEC.md
├── src/
│   ├── main.tsx
│   ├── App.tsx
│   ├── components/
│   ├── state/
│   ├── lib/
│   └── views/
├── src-tauri/
│   ├── Cargo.toml
│   ├── tauri.conf.json
│   └── src/
├── package.json
├── vite.config.ts
├── CLAUDE.md
└── README.md
```

---

## References

- [Anthropic: Effective Harnesses for Long-Running Agents](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents)
- [PatternFly 6 Documentation](https://www.patternfly.org/)
- [Tauri 2.0 Documentation](https://v2.tauri.app/)
- [portable-pty (Rust)](https://docs.rs/portable-pty)

---

## Success Metrics

| Metric                     | Target         |
|----------------------------|----------------|
| App startup time           | < 2s           |
| Log rendering latency      | < 16ms (60fps) |
| PTY input latency          | < 50ms         |
| Memory usage (10 projects) | < 200MB        |
| Binary size                | < 30MB         |

content.text:
## HQ - O Que Faz

**HQ (Headquarters)** é uma aplicação desktop nativa para **gerenciar sessões de desenvolvimento com agentes de IA** (como Claude Code). É essencialmente um "painel de controle" visual.

### Problema que Resolve

Quando você usa Claude Code para tarefas longas:
- Cada sessão começa **sem memória** das anteriores
- Múltiplos projetos são difíceis de acompanhar no terminal
- Não há visão consolidada do progresso

### Solução

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ HQ Desktop App (Tauri + React 19 + PatternFly 6)           │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  [Tab Bar]     │  [Kanban Board]   │  [Logs Panel]         │
│  Projetos      │  Features         │  Output em tempo real │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│            Rust Backend (PTY + File Watcher)               │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Features Principais

| Feature | Função |
|---------|--------|
| **Multi-project tabs** | Gerenciar vários projetos simultaneamente |
| **Kanban visual** | Visualizar features (BACKLOG → DONE) |
| **Logs real-time** | Output de processos com cores ANSI |
| **PTY Controller** | Controlar shells (Ctrl+C, resize, etc) |
| **Harness state** | Visualizar `progress.txt` e `features.json` |

---

## Como Faz (Arquitetura)

### Stack
- **Tauri 2.x** - Shell nativo + backend Rust
- **React 19** - Frontend
- **PatternFly 6** - UI components (LogViewer, Tabs, Chat)
- **@zazcart/tools** - State machine (evita hooks do React)

### Harness Pattern

O projeto usa um **Harness** - framework para IA trabalhar em tarefas longas:

```
.harness/
├── progress.txt    # Histórico de sessões
└── features.json   # Features com status (passes: true/false)
```

O Rust backend observa esses arquivos e atualiza a UI automaticamente.

### Estado Atual

- **0/10 features completas**
- Próximos passos: F009 (state machine), F010 (verificar Tauri), F001 (tabs)
- Estrutura base criada, dependências instaladas

# HQ - Harness Orchestrator & Dashboard

## Vision

**HQ** (Headquarters) is a native desktop application that provides a visual dashboard for managing AI agent development sessions. It enables developers to orchestrate, monitor, and control multiple Claude Code sessions across different projects without using a terminal.

## Core Philosophy

> "A Single Pane of Glass for AI-Assisted Development"

HQ transforms the way developers interact with long-running AI agents by providing:
- **Visual Kanban boards** for feature tracking
- **Real-time logs** from all running processes
- **PTY-based process control** with full interactivity
- **Multi-project management** via tabs
- **Harness state visualization**

---

## Architecture Overview

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                         HQ Desktop App                               │
│                    (Tauri + React 19 + PatternFly 6)                 │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                                                      │
│  ┌────────────────┐  ┌────────────────┐  ┌────────────────────────┐ │
│  │   Tab Bar      │  │   Kanban View   │  │   Logs Panel           │ │
│  │   (Projects)   │  │   (Features)    │  │   (Real-time PTY)      │ │
│  └────────────────┘  └────────────────┘  └────────────────────────┘ │
│                                                                      │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                       State Management Layer                         │
│                  (@zazcart/tools state-machine)                    │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                       Rust Backend (Tauri)                           │
│                                                                      │
│  ┌─────────────────┐  ┌─────────────────┐  ┌─────────────────────┐  │
│  │ PTY Controller  │  │ File Watcher    │  │ IPC Commands        │  │
│  │ (portable-pty)  │  │ (notify crate)  │  │ (Tauri invoke)      │  │
│  └─────────────────┘  └─────────────────┘  └─────────────────────┘  │
│                                                                      │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                     External Processes                               │
│                                                                      │
│  ┌─────────────────┐  ┌─────────────────┐  ┌─────────────────────┐  │
│  │ Claude Code #1  │  │ Claude Code #2  │  │ Other Processes     │  │
│  │ (Project A)     │  │ (Project B)     │  │ (servers, tests)    │  │
│  └─────────────────┘  └─────────────────┘  └─────────────────────┘  │
│                                                                      │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## Technology Stack

| Layer                | Technology          | Version | Purpose                              |
|----------------------|---------------------|---------|--------------------------------------|
| **Desktop Runtime**  | Tauri               | 2.x     | Native app shell, Rust backend       |
| **Frontend**         | React               | 19.x    | UI framework                         |
| **UI Components**    | PatternFly          | 6.x     | Enterprise UI (chat, terminal, logs) |
| **State Management** | @zazcart/tools      | 0.1.0   | State machine + schema validation    |
| **PTY Emulation**    | portable-pty (Rust) | latest  | Process spawning and control         |
| **File Watching**    | notify (Rust)       | latest  | Real-time harness file changes       |
| **IPC**              | Tauri Commands      | -       | Frontend ↔ Backend communication     |

### Why PatternFly 6?

PatternFly provides ready-made components for our use cases:
- **Chat/AI components** - For agent communication UI
- **Log viewer** - Real-time log display with ANSI support
- **Terminal** - Embedded terminal views
- **Tabs** - Multi-project navigation
- **Cards/Panels** - Kanban board layout
- **Dark theme** - Built-in dark mode support

---

## Feature Specifications

### F001: Multi-Project Tab Management

**Description:** Open and manage multiple projects simultaneously via tabs.

**UI Elements:**
- Tab bar at top (PatternFly Tabs)
- New tab button (+)
- Close tab button (X)
- Tab context menu (rename, close others, close all)

**Behavior:**
- Each tab represents a project directory
- Tab title shows project name (from package.json or folder name)
- Active tab is highlighted
- Tabs are reorderable via drag-and-drop

### F002: Kanban Board View

**Description:** Visual kanban board for feature tracking within a project.

**Columns:**
```
BACKLOG → TODO → DOING → REVIEW → QA → DONE
```

**Card Structure:**
- Feature ID (e.g., F001)
- Title
- Description (truncated)
- Status badge
- Priority indicator
- Click to expand details

**Data Source:**
- Reads from `.harness/features.json`
- Auto-updates when file changes (via file watcher)
- Status mapped to columns:
  - `passes: false` + not started → BACKLOG
  - `passes: false` + priority → TODO
  - `passes: false` + in_progress → DOING
  - `passes: false` + needs_review → REVIEW
  - `passes: false` + testing → QA
  - `passes: true` → DONE

### F003: Real-Time Log Panel

**Description:** Live output from running processes (Claude Code, servers, etc.)

**Features:**
- PatternFly LogViewer component
- ANSI color support
- Timestamp prefix
- Process filter
- Search within logs
- Clear button
- Auto-scroll toggle

**Technical:**
- PTY streams → Tauri events → React state
- Max 10,000 lines per process (ring buffer)

### F004: PTY Process Controller

**Description:** Spawn, control, and interact with shell processes.

**Capabilities:**
- Start new process (e.g., `claude` command)
- Send keystrokes (including Ctrl+C, Ctrl+D)
- Resize PTY on window resize
- Kill process
- Restart process

**Rust Implementation:**
```rust
use portable_pty::{CommandBuilder, PtySize, native_pty_system};

fn spawn_process(cmd: &str, cols: u16, rows: u16) -> Result<PtyPair> {
    let pty_system = native_pty_system();
    let pair = pty_system.openpty(PtySize { rows, cols, .. })?;
    let cmd = CommandBuilder::new(cmd);
    pair.slave.spawn_command(cmd)?;
    Ok(pair)
}
```

### F005: Harness State Visualization

**Description:** Display current harness state visually.

**Components:**
- Progress summary card
  - Features completed / total
  - Current session info
  - Last commit
- Active feature highlight
- Session timeline (from progress.txt)

### F006: Project Selector Dialog

**Description:** Open new project from file system.

**UI:**
- PatternFly Modal
- Recent projects list
- Browse button (native file picker via Tauri)
- Validate project (check for .harness folder)

### F007: Settings Panel

**Description:** Configure HQ preferences.

**Settings:**
- Theme (light/dark/system)
- Log buffer size
- Auto-start Claude Code
- Default shell
- Font size

---

## State Schema (using @zazcart/tools)

```typescript
import { Type, Static } from 'typebox';
import { createStore } from '@zazcart/tools';

// Project Schema
const ProjectSchema = Type.Object({
  id: Type.String(),
  path: Type.String(),
  name: Type.String(),
  harness: Type.Optional(Type.Object({
    progressFile: Type.String(),
    featuresFile: Type.String(),
  })),
});

// Feature Schema (from harness)
const FeatureSchema = Type.Object({
  id: Type.String(),
  description: Type.String(),
  category: Type.String(),
  passes: Type.Boolean(),
  steps: Type.Array(Type.String()),
});

// Process Schema
const ProcessSchema = Type.Object({
  id: Type.String(),
  projectId: Type.String(),
  command: Type.String(),
  status: Type.Union([
    Type.Literal('running'),
    Type.Literal('stopped'),
    Type.Literal('error'),
  ]),
  pid: Type.Optional(Type.Number()),
});

// App State
const AppStateSchema = Type.Object({
  activeTab: Type.String(),
  theme: Type.Union([
    Type.Literal('light'),
    Type.Literal('dark'),
    Type.Literal('system'),
  ]),
  projects: Type.Record(Type.String(), ProjectSchema),
  projectOrder: Type.Array(Type.String()),
  features: Type.Record(Type.String(), Type.Array(FeatureSchema)),
  processes: Type.Record(Type.String(), ProcessSchema),
});

type AppState = Static<typeof AppStateSchema>;
```

---

## Tauri Commands (IPC)

```typescript
// Frontend → Backend commands
interface TauriCommands {
  'project:open': (path: string) => Promise<Project>;
  'project:close': (id: string) => Promise<void>;
  'pty:spawn': (projectId: string, cmd: string) => Promise<string>;
  'pty:write': (processId: string, data: string) => Promise<void>;
  'pty:resize': (processId: string, cols: number, rows: number) => Promise<void>;
  'pty:kill': (processId: string) => Promise<void>;
  'harness:read_features': (path: string) => Promise<Feature[]>;
  'harness:watch': (projectPath: string) => Promise<void>;
  'fs:select_directory': () => Promise<string | null>;
}

// Backend → Frontend events
interface TauriEvents {
  'pty:output': { processId: string; data: string };
  'pty:exit': { processId: string; code: number };
  'harness:changed': { projectPath: string; file: string };
}
```

---

## Directory Structure

```
~/antonio/HQ/
├── .claude/
│   ├── settings.json
│   └── hooks/
├── .harness/
│   ├── progress.txt
│   └── features.json
├── docs/
│   └── HQ_SPEC.md
├── src/
│   ├── main.tsx
│   ├── App.tsx

│   ├── lib/

├── src-tauri/

│   ├── tauri.conf.json
│   └── src/
├── package.json
├── vite.config.ts
├── CLAUDE.md
└── README.md
```

---

## References

- [Anthropic: Effective Harnesses for Long-Running Agents](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents)
- [PatternFly 6 Documentation](https://www.patternfly.org/)
- [Tauri 2.0 Documentation](https://v2.tauri.app/)
- [portable-pty (Rust)](https://docs.rs/portable-pty)

---

## Success Metrics

| Metric                     | Target         |
|----------------------------|----------------|
| App startup time           | < 2s           |
| Log rendering latency      | < 16ms (60fps) |
| PTY input latency          | < 50ms         |
| Memory usage (10 projects) | < 200MB        |
| Binary size                | < 30MB         |

# HQ

Native desktop application (Tauri + React 19 + PatternFly 6) for managing AI agent development sessions. It provides visual task boards, real-time logs, and PTY-based process control.

---

## HARNESS: Fundamental Concept (READ THIS FIRST)

> **This project uses a Harness.** If you don't know what that is, STOP and research:
> - [Effective harnesses for long-running agents](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents)

### What is a Harness?

A **Harness** is a framework that enables AI agents to work on long tasks across multiple context windows/sessions.

**Problem:** Each new Claude Code session starts with NO memory of previous sessions.

**Solution:** State files persist context between sessions:
- `./harness/claude-progress.txt` → session history
- `./harness/features.json` → requirements with status (passes: true/false)

### Work Protocol

```
1. READ ./harness/claude-progress.txt (understand current state)
2. READ ./harness/features.json (find next feature)
3. IMPLEMENT one feature
4. TEST visually and interactively (Playwright MCP)
5. COMMIT after passing
6. UPDATE claude-progress.txt
7. STOP (leave codebase production-ready)
```

### Self-Correction Protocol

If you don't understand what harness is after reading this:
1. **SEARCH:** WebSearch "Anthropic harness agents 2026"
2. **ANALYZE:** Why was local documentation unclear?
3. **FIX:** Update CLAUDE.md or hooks to improve clarity
4. **DOCUMENT:** Record the fix in claude-progress.txt

### Harness Files

| File                  | Purpose              | When to Read       |
|-----------------------|----------------------|--------------------|
| `claude-progress.txt` | Session history      | **ALWAYS first**   |
| `features.json`       | Features with status | After progress.txt |
| `CLAUDE.md`           | Project rules        | Reference          |

---

You cannot see the app you're building. Without visual testing, you will:

- Miss CSS/layout issues
- Overlook runtime errors visible only in UI

### Testing Protocol: Playwright MCP

Use **Playwright MCP** for visual tests. Runs headless and **does not steal focus** from the user's window.

#### Step 1: Navigate to the page

```
mcp__playwright__browser_navigate url="file:///full/path/to/file.html"
```

Or for local server:
```
mcp__playwright__browser_navigate url="http://localhost:5173"
```

#### Step 2: Check console (JS errors)

```
mcp__playwright__browser_console_messages level="error"
```

- Red errors → **FAIL** (investigate before continuing)
- Yellow warnings → Evaluate (e.g., "Tailwind CDN production warning" is OK)

#### Step 3: Capture screenshot

```
mcp__playwright__browser_take_screenshot filename="screenshots/test-name.png"
```

For scrollable pages:
```
mcp__playwright__browser_take_screenshot fullPage=true filename="screenshots/test-full.png"
```

#### Step 4: Analyze visually

Check in the screenshot:
- [ ] Content visible? (not blank/empty)
- [ ] Layout correct? (elements positioned as expected)
- [ ] Colors correct? (matching design)
- [ ] No visual artifacts?

#### Step 5: Interactions (if needed)

```
mcp__playwright__browser_click element="button X" ref="e123"
mcp__playwright__browser_type element="field Y" ref="e456" text="value"
```

#### Step 6: Close browser at the end

```
mcp__playwright__browser_close
```

### Approval Criteria

| Criterion              | PASS | FAIL                      |
|------------------------|------|---------------------------|
| Console without errors | ✅    | ❌ Red errors              |
| Content rendered       | ✅    | ❌ Blank/empty screen      |
| Layout correct         | ✅    | ❌ Misaligned elements     |
| JS executed            | ✅    | ❌ Dynamic content missing |

### Final Decision

```
Visual OK + Console clean    → passes: true
Visual OK + Console warnings → Evaluate (may pass)
Visual OK + Console errors   → FAIL (fix it)
Visual broken                → FAIL (fix it)
```

### What is a Harness?

A **Harness** is a framework that enables AI agents to work on long tasks across multiple context windows/sessions.

**Problem:** Each new Claude Code session starts with NO memory of previous sessions.

**Solution:** State files persist context between sessions:
- `.harness/progress.txt` → session history
- `.harness/features.json` → requirements with status (passes: true/false)

### Work Protocol

```
1. READ .harness/progress.txt (understand current state)
2. READ .harness/features.json (find next feature with passes: false)
3. IMPLEMENT one feature
4. TEST visually (run the app)
5. COMMIT after passing
6. UPDATE .harness/progress.txt
7. STOP (leave codebase production-ready)
```

## HARNESS: Fundamental Concept

> **This project uses a Harness.** If you don't know what that is:
> - [Effective harnesses for long-running agents](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents)

### What is a Harness?

A **Harness** is a framework that enables AI agents to work on long tasks across multiple context windows/sessions.

**Problem:** Each new Claude Code session starts with NO memory of previous sessions.

**Solution:** State files persist context between sessions:
- `.harness/progress.txt` → session history
- `.harness/features.json` → requirements with status (passes: true/false)

### Work Protocol

```
1. READ .harness/progress.txt (understand current state)
2. READ .harness/features.json (find next feature with passes: false)
3. IMPLEMENT one feature
4. TEST visually (run the app)
5. COMMIT after passing
6. UPDATE .harness/progress.txt
7. STOP (leave codebase production-ready)
```
